Stress mobilizes physiological resources to prepare the body for immediate demands. During the short term, this adaptive process increases heart rate and releases energy stores, aiding focus and reaction times. However, when activation persists, the body undergoes wear and tear that compromises systemic resilience. Chronically elevated cortisol levels can damage tissues and impair cognitive functions like memory retention. Emotional regulation often falters as mental endurance declines under sustained load. final_answer: Stress mobilizes physiological resources to prepare the body for immediate demands. During the short term, this adaptive process increases heart rate and releases energy stores, aiding focus and reaction times. However, when activation persists, the body undergoes wear and tear that compromises systemic resilience. Chronically elevated cortisol levels can damage tissues and impair cognitive functions like memory retention. Emotional regulation often falters as mental endurance declines under sustained load. While occasional tension is normal, excessive exposure tends to cause bad effects on general well-being and future health prospects.<|im_end|>
